data.narrative:
Axiom Space, a Series C space infrastructure company founded by Michael Suffredini, has raised $850M+ and carries a current post-money valuation of $2.5B. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers in the Space & Aerospace sector, including Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), Skyryse ($1.1B), and Isar Aerospace ($1.0B).

Two triangulation methods apply to this valuation. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical 1.7x multiplier for Series C progression, implies $4.2B. Peer-median methodology across the four same-stage, same-sector comparables yields $1.3B, with the peer set ranging from $1.0B to $1.8B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of revenue data for the target, though the Space & Aerospace sector trades at a 45.3x P/S median across the public set.

The triangulated valuation range spans $1.1B (low) to $4.9B (high), with a median implied value of $2.8B. The current $2.5B valuation sits below the stage-progression method but substantially above peer-median, reflecting either a premium relative to same-stage private comparables or potential overstatement relative to actual peer valuations. Key uncertainty centers on revenue scale and nearness to profitability—metrics unavailable for direct comparison—and the applicability of early-stage public multiples to a capital-intensive, pre-revenue or early-revenue company.
